title: hemigenia canescens
intro: Hemigenia canescens, also known as the woolly hemigenia, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hemigenia canescens include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Hemigenia canescens can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
